we're trying to do with this strategy is, first and foremost, we want to establish visibility in AI tools like ChatGPT, perplexity.

There are a lot of keyboard opportunities that we could go after, but we want to understand, okay, what.
Has the most traffic, so that I put together a list of the top volume opportunities with over 10,000 monthly searches.
Keep in mind that this is based not, so for example, if you look up SOX compliance in SEMrush, it's not going to have this search volume.
This is based on the overall search volume of all of the keywords that competitor content ranks for that's related to SOX compliance.

And kind of see the sub-sub-topics.
And we used all of these, we used all of this information, we kind of synthesized it to arrive at six topic clusters.
So we take a topic cluster approach to content strategies because we don't want to do a spray and pray approach.
We don't want to be randomly publishing content.
We do topic clusters for, I think, nearly every single one of our clients.
And it works well because it allows us to build authority in specific areas mindfully.
And it allows us to make sure, okay, like, did we really cover this topic in depth?
Is there anything else we could add to it?
So a topic cluster can cover multiple stages of the funnel.
Some topic clusters may be more like commercial, but the ones that I have suggested, they can span both commercial and informational keywords.
And, you know, focusing on just six for now.
Well,
Allows us to kind of make sure that we're targeting like the right priorities.
When I was trying to brainstorm these clusters, one thing that stands out is that there are a lot of topics that we could be potentially covering here.
So I was, you know, on your website, I was just browsing and I realized you have all of these different solutions laid out really well on your site.

So my idea was, let's map clusters to solutions so that every single cluster, first of all, every single blog post that we publish in every cluster, we have immediately a solutions page that we can link it to.
So we're automatically building like internal linking, we're also giving users a path to go if they're interested in learning more.
And it also signals like it allows us to build authority in LLMs because we can very organically bring up like risk advisory in a blog post about, you know.
So internal audits, and it doesn't feel like it's shoehorned or anything like that.
So that was my idea, open to feedback, obviously, but this is how I, I think it allows us to kind of cover everything without going like too broad.
So the six topic clusters are, the first one is AI-powered audit automation.
So this one isn't, it's kind of covering multiple solutions, and it's the only one that does that.
So we want to cover like more higher funnel keywords like AI and risk management, AI for fraud detection.
But this cluster would also target very commercial keywords like Onspring versus Audit Board versus Fieldguide, best of listicles, alternatives.
And I know that not everyone is a fan of these types of blog posts, especially when it comes to like mentioning competitors on your website.
However, these types of blog posts.
really do well in AI search.
They get cited by different LLMs.
